title: Reproducible Dependency Update Entry
description: One reproducible dependency update across ecosystems. Shared contract for RP 2025/2026 Q4 — extending BUMP across ecosystems and update categories. v0.0.5 replaces the scalar `environmentFingerprint` with a list `environmentFingerprints`, each tagged by container platform (e.g. linux/arm64, linux/amd64). This lets a single entry accumulate cross-architecture verifications: each arch has its own digest because the fat-image `packages.txt` and `rustc.txt` differ by arch, but the reproduction contract (same apt snapshot, same SDE, same rust) is arch-agnostic. v0.0.4 renamed the commit fields from breaking-centric (preBreaking/breaking) to category-neutral (pre/post) and added an optional `fix` commit for fix-after-update entries.

category.description: Update category. Extends BUMP with non-breaking and fix-after-update; unreproducible is included so drop-outs are preserved for RQ1 drop-out analysis.

commits.description: Commit SHAs defining the reproduction frames. `pre` = before the dependency update (baseline, tests expected to pass). `post` = after the update (for breaking: tests expected to fail; for non-breaking: tests expected to pass). `fix` = optional, present only for fix-after-update entries; tests expected to pass again.

scope.description: Ecosystem-specific scope: runtime, dev, build, test, etc.

reproduction.description: Present only when the entry is reproduced (category != unreproducible). Records the regeneration contract, not image references.

fatImage.description: Inputs the regenerator needs to rebuild the fat toolchain image that all thin layers (pre/post/fix) sit on top of.

rustVersion.description: Full patch version of rustc, e.g. '1.92.0'. Must be what the rust:X.Y.Z-<release> base image provides.

sourceDateEpoch.description: Unix timestamp used as SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH when building the fat image. Also controls the apt snapshot URL.

aptSnapshot.description: Snapshot.debian.org timestamp, e.g. '20260415T000000Z'. Redundant with sourceDateEpoch but kept human-readable.

expectedDigest.description: OCI manifest digest observed when the entry was first validated. ADVISORY: mismatch is reported as a warning, not a hard fail, because of known apt-layer byte jitter (see docs/cargo/reproducible-builds.md).

buildFlags.description: Exact flags passed to `cargo test` / equivalent build command. Part of the reproducibility contract.

environmentFingerprints.description: Environmental equivalence contract (Fork B), per container platform. The regenerator rebuilds the fat image, extracts /manifest/*, and asserts the entry for the current host's container arch matches byte-for-byte. New arches get appended on first verification rather than rejected. Each entry's `digest` is authoritative for its `platform`.

platform.description: Container platform, e.g. linux/arm64, linux/amd64. Distinct from verifiedOn[].platform (which records the host OS+arch); fingerprints are keyed by the Linux-container arch the fat image ran as.

digest.description: sha256 of the concatenation of files listed in `files`, in the order listed. Authoritative for this platform.

files.description: Per-file hashes of /manifest/* inside the fat image, in concat order. Used for diagnostic diffs when `digest` mismatches.

rustcVersion.description: Copy of /manifest/rustc.txt first line, for human glanceability. Not used for comparison.

thinImages.description: Advisory digests for the per-entry thin images. MISMATCH IS NOT A FAIL — thin-image byte reproducibility is not in scope. Recorded for cross-host comparison studies. `expectedFix` present only for fix-after-update entries.

verifiedOn.description: Platforms + hosts the reproduction contract was verified on. Empty on first ingestion; appended to by the regenerator.

host.description: Free-text host label, e.g. 'macbook-local', 'tudelft-ci'. Helpful for cross-host studies.

outcomeMatch.description: Did the regeneration reproduce the recorded pass/fail outcome? Null if not attempted.

failure.description: Present when reproduction confirmed a failing build (breaking entries or the pre-fix step of fix-after-update entries).

subCategory.description: Ecosystem-specific subcategory. Each ecosystem owns its own enum (see failure-taxonomy.md).

errorCodes.description: Raw ecosystem-specific error codes, e.g. rustc E0277, Maven enforcer rule names.

ecosystemMetadata.description: Open escape hatch for ecosystem-specific fields that do not warrant top-level status. Consumers may ignore.

unreproducibilityReason.description: Present when category == unreproducible. Short free-text reason.
